description_long: This object consists of three ornately decorated, stacked books with brown covers and gold detailing, accompanied by three oranges, one of which is sliced in half to reveal its juicy, segmented interior. The books feature intricate embossed patterns on their covers and spines, with one book partially open and standing upright. The oranges display a natural, slightly textured rind, with the sliced orange showing a vibrant, realistic cross-section. The books and fruit are arranged closely together, creating a visually rich composition that contrasts the organic forms of the oranges with the geometric, structured books. The overall appearance suggests a blend of literary and natural elements, possibly for decorative or thematic purposes. The materials and textures are rendered with attention to detail, highlighting the tactile qualities of both the books and the fruit.
description: A stack of three ornate, brown books with gold detailing is paired with three realistic oranges, one sliced open to reveal its interior. The composition contrasts the textured fruit with the embossed, structured books.
description_view_0: Two whole oranges and one sliced orange are clearly visible next to a stack of three decorated books, with one book partially open and standing upright.
description_view_1: The front cover of the top book is fully visible, showing detailed embossing, while the oranges are partially obscured behind the books.
description_view_2: The spines of the books with gold patterns are prominent, and the oranges are positioned to the right, with the open book leaning to the left.
description_view_3: Three oranges are in the foreground, with the books stacked behind them, and the open book is less visible from this angle.
attribute_location_or_placement: library table, study desk, or decorative shelf
